response: THE FIRST INSTALLATION UTILIZES A PARAMETRIC EQUATION TO GENERATE A DYNAMIC GEOMETRIC PATTERN ON A LARGE SCREEN. FOR THE PATTERN TO FORM A CLOSED LOOP, THE FUNCTION MUST REPEAT ITS VALUES AFTER A CERTAIN PERIOD T. THIS OCCURS WHEN THE ARGUMENTS OF THE TRIGONOMETRIC FUNCTIONS COMPLETE AN INTEGER NUMBER OF CYCLES. THE PERIOD OF THE FUNCTION COS(AT + Φ) + COS(BT) AND SIN(AT + Φ) + SIN(BT) DEPENDS ON THE RATIO OF A AND B. TO ENSURE THE PATTERN IS A CLOSED LOOP, THE RATIO OF A TO B MUST BE A RATIONAL NUMBER. LET A = P/Q AND B = R/S WHERE P, Q, R, S ARE INTEGERS. IF A AND B ARE IN RATIONAL PROPORTION, THEN THE PERIOD T IS THE LEAST COMMON MULTIPLE OF THE INDIVIDUAL PERIODS OF THE COSINES AND SINES. THE PERIOD OF COS(AT + Φ) IS 2π / A, AND THE PERIOD OF COS(BT) IS 2π / B. THE LEAST COMMON MULTIPLE OF THESE PERIODS IS 2π / GCD(A, B) WHERE GCD(A, B) IS THE GREATEST COMMON DIVISOR OF A AND B. THE PHASE SHIFT Φ DOES NOT AFFECT THE PERIOD, ONLY THE POSITION OF THE LOOP. THEREFORE, THE CONDITIONS ARE THAT A AND B MUST BE RATIONAL NUMBERS, AND THE PERIOD T IS 2π / GCD(A, B). GOOD ART IS CREATED WHEN THE CONDITIONS ARE MET, AND THE PATTERN REPEATS PERFECTLY. THE SECOND INSTALLATION INVOLVES A 3D SURFACE DESCRIBED BY THE EQUATION X² + Y² + Z² + Λ(XY + YZ + ZX) - 1 = 0. THIS EQUATION REPRESENTS A QUADRIC SURFACE, AND THE VALUE OF Λ DETERMINES ITS SHAPE. FOR Λ = 0, THE EQUATION SIMPLIFIES TO THE UNIT SPHERE, A PERFECTLY SYMMETRICAL OBJECT. AS Λ INCREASES, THE SURFACE BECOMES MORE ELLIPSOIDAL AND CAN UNDERGO TOPOLOGICAL CHANGES. THE CRITICAL VALUES OF Λ OCCUR WHEN THE
